working_definition: A terminally differentiated vascular endothelial cell found in the arteriolar branch of the dermal vasculature in prenatal human skin (7-17 PCW). Arterioles represent the final differentiation state along the arteriolar trajectory (early endothelial cell -> capillary -> capillary arteriole -> arteriole) and are marked by expression of GJA5 (gap junction protein alpha-5). Their full differentiation from capillary arteriole precursors requires immune cell-derived signals.

best_match.cl_definition: An endothelial cell that is part of an arteriole.

justification: CL:1000412 'endothelial cell of arteriole' is the generic, tissue-unspecific CL term for endothelial cells of arterioles, and is an exact match for the 'Arterioles' cell type in this atlas. The atlas annotates these cells by their GJA5-expressing arteriolar identity in prenatal skin vasculature. The other arteriole endothelial hits (CL:1001096 kidney afferent arteriole endothelial cell, CL:1001099 kidney efferent arteriole endothelial cell) are kidney-specific and do not apply to skin. CL:1000412 is the appropriate parent term covering arteriole endothelial cells in any tissue.

other_candidates.cl_definition: An endothelial cell that is part of a kidney afferent arteriole.
other_candidates.reason_rejected: Kidney-specific; the atlas context is prenatal skin

other_candidates.cl_definition: An endothelial cell that is part of a kidney efferent arteriole.
other_candidates.reason_rejected: Kidney-specific; the atlas context is prenatal skin

recommendation: Map to CL:1000412 (endothelial cell of arteriole). No new CL term is needed.
